programmes.show_description: This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Sales Master. Tish and her dad have set up a yard sale, but Tish is having no luck selling anything. When a customer shows up, Tish fails to win them over.
Skateboard. Children's animation. Bing can't wait to have a go on a skateboard, but it's much harder to balance than Pando made it look.
Lullaby. Preschool series. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to sing Tiddlytubby RuRu a lullaby.
Sea. Tee is excited about a day on the beach but soon finds out that the sea is a bit... wet. It's now up to Mo to teach Tee that splashing in the water is fun.
Operation Kiwi. Supertato and the Veggies follow a noise in the plant aisle and meet a famous explorer.
Charlie Did It. Bing teaches Charlie how to throw but accidentally knocks the fruit over.
UFO. Mojo and Bo only wanted to help fix a satellite dish with Mojo's chopper top. Now they've been mistaken for a UFO, and the whole town is in a tizzy about invading aliens!
Potato Time. At Squirrel Club, Duggee forgets how to woof, Duggly comes to visit, Roly celebrates Potato Time, and Billy teaches everyone the Bugatron dance!
The Topiary Badge. After seeing Duggee's topiary skills, the Squirrels are keen to try it out for themselves.
Space. Mackenzie, Jack and Rusty are playing astronauts on a mission to Mars!
Hammerbarn. On a trip to Hammerbarn, Bluey and Bingo create their own mini-homes side-by-side in the trolley. But Bluey quickly gets jealous of her neighbour Bingo.
Go Jet Academy: New Species. The team go in search of a mysterious new creature on the Academy Island.
The Tale of the Locked Cage. Animated series. When Mr McGregor locks him in a cage with Sammy Whiskers, Peter must join forces with the rat to escape.
Desert Creatures. Kwazii and Periwinkle try to help a bird found in the Sonoran Desert.
Two. The animated adventures of friends who can always count on each other. Two finds a pair of magic dancing shoes and shows One that everything is better with two.
Kick. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. K can kick but C can't - so it's word-magic-for-goalposts and a kid keeping score.
Favourite Library Book Day. Love Monster and Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny try to find Love Monster's favourite book.
Tubes. Bella and Lift enjoy making funny honk noises with tubes. They visit Tincy the Spider, who uses terrific tubes to make music with the Playtimers.
Lychee. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's love and peace symbol Lychee performing his song All You Need Is Lychee!
Hose Pipe. Bing turns the garden hose on too fast, turning it into a big snake!
Rain. Dee encourages a friend to say 'rain', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see lots of rain - drinking rain, stormy rain, rainbow rain and even banana rain.
Counting Spaghetti. Lu and Barnaby have a disagreement during art time at Shell School.
Bunny Builders School. The Piggies try to teach three bunnies how to build things.
More About Louder and Louder. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos are making a lot of noise in the kitchen - and they seem to be getting louder and louder.
Farmyard Fun. Meet some cute alpacas with Justin and his friend as they visit a farm, while Mr Tumble, Grandad Tumble and Aunt Polly sing Old MacDonald at the farmyard dance.
Party Invitation. Preschool series. A little girl makes birthday party invitations. The Teletubbies receive a magical party invitation from the Tiddlytubbies and do party dancing.
Bedtime. Giovanna and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore bedtime toys and objects through discovery, play, song and story. Babies and grown-ups at home can join in too!
Beach. Elsee plays I-spy on a day out at the beach with her friends Indie and Rosie.
Trees. Adrian, Mia and Aaron go on a 'tree-mendous' garden adventure to discover the magical powers of trees.
Time Machine. Rebecca and Murphi are planning to go on a travelling adventure, so they use straws to make a sailboat. They also create a paper plate dinosaur to keep them company!
Perfect Pair. The gang help a lost Avocadodon find its perfect pair with a head-rubbing ritual.
Spy Game. Bingo stops playing Bluey's spy game in the park.
The Babysitting Badge. Duggee has a job to do outside the Clubhouse, but who will look after the Squirrels? King Tiger, of course, since he has his babysitting badge!

programmes.show_name: Hey Duggee: Top of the Pups
programmes.show_description: Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush. Katerina Flamingo sings a song about routines in Here We Go Round the Mulberry Bush.
Wishing Well and Owls Walk. On a walk to play a game with sticks, Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers find a wishing well and fly like owls!
Iona and Elba's Ice Cream Parlour. Iona and Elba make some delicious ice creams with loads of sprinkles! Who wants one?
Jessica's Ukrainian Borscht. Jessica is making a traditional soup from Ukraine, and it's made with bright beetroot.

programmes.show_name: Teeny Tiny Creatures with Chantelle and Rory
programmes.show_description: Beetles. Chantelle searches for one of nature's recyclers. Rory and Sandy are on a beetle hunt.
Mission: Big Bear. Therapy dog Sylvie uses her superheart to help the children on their first day at school.
Coral and Flamboyant Flower Beetle. Aneeshwar and Lizzie see coral and meet flamboyant flower beetles.
Waffle and the Box. A mysterious box is delivered to Mrs Hobbs' house, and Waffle wants to know what's inside.
The Foggy Day. Oona and Baba get lost in the fog. Mossy and the Shrew Crew help them find their way home.
You Can Be What You Want to Be. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. The Small Potatoes explore different jobs, from firemen to astronauts, in this disco pageant.
Sound of Silence. Yukee enjoys the peace and quiet in the garden when she wakes up early. But she soon discovers that while it might be quiet, the garden is never silent.
The Tree Badge. Duggee helps the Squirrels learn about all the amazing things trees do for the creatures who live among them, and earn their tree badges in the process!
Hair. Dee encourages a friend to say 'hair', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see all different types of hair - brown hair, pink hair and even dogs with funny hair!
The Great Bug Race. It's Numberland's Great Bug Race! One to Four's pet bugs are racing on special tracks, but can they follow the perimeter?
Alphabet. Educational fun. When the Alphablocks make the alphabet, anything can happen - as PH, WH and a rollerskating elephant find out!
Down on the Farm. Justin and his friends meet a chicken, while Mr Tumble finds a worm.
Music. Preschool series. The Teletubbies become a pop group and perform a song. In Tummy Tales, children watch a one-man band playing music and accompany him by playing their own.
Patchwork Chick. Patchwork Chick has a problem: nobody wants to play with him because he is so small! But aren't there even smaller Patchwork Pals around who also want to play?
Crocky's Wobbly Tooth. Stop-frame animation featuring Raa Raa, who lives in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. Crocky really wants his wobbly tooth to fall out.
The Spooky Chugger. Rattling Rivets! The Spooky Chugger has returned! With unexplained happenings around Chuggington, the trainees are convinced the tracks are haunted.
Episode 20. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Rapids Johnson dons his bear disguise in search of the lesser-spotted copying bear.
Leptictidium and Fig. Adventure series. Andy searches for the tiny leptictidium, a furry mammal that used to bounce around the forests of Europe fifty million years ago.
The Upside-Down Map. The Game Catchers land on the Perfume Planet and learn to play the train race.
Immortal Jellyfish. Animated deep sea adventures. Shellington and the Octonauts try to track an unusual creature, a jellyfish that can live forever.
The Tale of Old Rusty. Animated series. Peter and friends venture to Rocky Island to find the mysterious Old Rusty mentioned in Peter's father's journal.
The Cheese Badge. Duggee and the Squirrels go on a mission to contain an outbreak.
Fergie's Sad Day. When Fergie is feeling off, Vida's diagnosis is that she's having a sad day.
Galactic Space Showdown. Supertato and Chilli are playing a computer game when Evil Pea takes it for herself. To get it back, they suddenly find themselves in a real version of the game.
Mission Egg. The ducklings find an egg, and they want Weasy to hatch it.
Pizzas Away. Mojo needs to deliver a pizza to a very hard-to-reach island, but some stubborn seabirds have their eye on the pizza. Will Mojo have to go supersonic to get past them?
Space. Mackenzie, Jack and Rusty are playing astronauts on a mission to Mars!
When You Wish Upon a Whale. Norman misses his whale friends. The Piris try to help, but his size proves a challenge.
Hairdressers. In a game of hairdressers, Bingo has to find her voice, while Bluey has to find her ears.
Andy Goes Fishing on Dino Island. Andy goes fishing on Dino Island to see what new prehistoric fish he can discover.
Waffle Tries to Be Still. Waffle ends up in Evie's class, where he must be still so the headteacher doesn't see him.
The Gingerbread Wish. Rafi's gingerbread wish brings her bakes to life. She discovers perfect can mean different things, and messy can be special too.
It's Time to Check the Weather Forecast. A rainy weather forecast threatens JoJo's plans to slide down the big slide at the park.
Balloon Barnacles. Animation for preschool children. Sarah's hat goes on an adventure without her, on top of a balloon.
The Discovery. Stop-motion animation. Major Clanger discovers some wall pictures in a cave and invites everyone to see them, but it turns out Small has seen the pictures before.
Mud. MeMo and BaBa find some fun and safe ways to play with glorious mud: stomping in puddles, making pies in a mud kitchen and planting some seeds with the Yummies.
Igglepiggle Looks for Upsy Daisy and Follows Her Bed. Children's series. Igglepiggle cannot find Upsy Daisy, but he spots her bed. Upsy Daisy's bed always knows where she is.
Dame Laura Kenny - Thank You. Legendary Olympian Dame Laura Kenny reads a happy and hopeful story about all the things to be thankful for.
This Is CBeebies. Programmes start at 6.00am.
Jungle Shoes. The family is going camping, but Tish doesn't want to get her new shoes muddy. To make matters worse, her parents then ask her to look after Bobby.
Butterfly. Children's animation. When a butterfly lands on Sula's painting, Bing tries to help it go back outside, but he holds it too tightly and the poor butterfly is squished.
Listen. Preschool series. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to listen to music through a pair of headphones. They love dancing to the music they hear.
Tee Can Do It. Mo encourages Tee to have a 'Tee can do it' day where he tries to do everything himself.
Museum Mischief. Evil Pea steals a mobile phone from the museum when pretending to help Supertato.
Helping Hoppity. Bing, Sula and Pando are playing superheroes in the park.
Ice Scream. Betty the ice cream van can't stop, so the chase is on for Mojo and Bo!
Song of Peace. The Squirrels hear the gerbils' peace song and push some buttons with Betty.
The A Cappella Badge. The Squirrels are inspired to learn how to sing a cappella style after hearing a famous bird quartet singing in a nearby tree.
Smoochy Kiss. Bluey and Bingo want Dad all to themselves and won't let Mum have a smoochy kiss!
Featherwand. Bingo is armed with a magic featherwand that makes things incredibly heavy.
Go Jetter or No Jetter? When Foz loses confidence, Tala shows that he still has what it takes to be a Go Jetter.
The Tale of Nutkin's Rabbity Day. Animated series. When the Squirrel Tribe forget his birthday, Nutkin quits the squirrel life and decides to become a rabbit.
Hawaiian Monk Seals. The Octonauts must repair a coral reef protecting a lagoon from surrounding sharks.
Three. The animated adventures of friends who can always count on each other. Learn all about the number three with Numberblock Three.
Cat. The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. A makes friends with a cat but soon starts sneezing and bouncing all over.
Happy to Help Day. Love Monster gets overwhelmed on Happy to Help Day after promising too much help!
Paper Fan. It's a hot day in Playtime Towers, so Bella helps Lift cool down with a paper fan. Together, they visit Hettie in the Ice Disco, who has a flapping paper fan dance!
Broccoli. Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Broccoli and his floret Broc performing their phenomenal ventriloquist act.
Birthday. It's Bing's third birthday, but there's trouble when he teaches his friends the waka-oke.
Toothbrush. Dee encourages a friend to say 'toothbrush', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure!
Lu's New Shoes. Lu's upset when no-one seems to like her new shoes.
Pie Party. Wilf calls the Piggies to keep them busy and steal their pie.
More About Pulling. Stop-motion preschool programme. The Twirlywoos help a man who is pulling his car out of the mud. When all the Twirlywoos pull together, they free the car.
Magic Castle. Justin and his friends visit a magical castle, and there's magic with The Great Tumbleeny!
Taking Turns. Preschool comedy and education. Laa-Laa and Po take turns to ride Po's scooter, and then they take turns to play with the Tiddlytubbies.
Farm. Nigel and Baby Bear invite little ones and their grown-ups to explore farm objects and toys through discovery, play, song and story. Babies and grown-ups at home can join in too!
Shopping Centre. Elsee visits a shopping centre with her friend George and plays a game of I-spy.
Tidying Up. James, Noah and Aaron are on a mission to tidy up the city centre garden for a party. They work alongside Chatterpot's friends - a rake, a spade and Wheely the wheelbarrow.
Healthy, Fruity Snacks. Rebecca and Murphi make silly eyeglasses and a train to help find their missing snacks.
Crystal Cavern. Ginger and the Peas embark on an underground chase in pursuit of a burrowing Aritchokus.
Takeaway. Forced to wait five minutes in front of a restaurant for their takeaway order, Bluey and Bingo become restless for entertainment, pushing the very limits of Dad's patience.
The Comic Badge. Duggee is reading the Squirrels one of his favourite comics. They are eager to know the ending, but the comic was discontinued!

programmes.show_name: Hey Duggee: Top of the Pups
programmes.show_description: Old MacDonald. Lord Fingal sings about all the animals as he takes the Squirrels on a tour around Old MacDonald's farm.
Cormorants and Compass Walk. Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers spot cormorants and play a game with colours on a walk to draw pictures!
Kyra and Khloe's Garden Fun. Kyra and Khloe get busy with some gardening before making their own garden juice bar!
Eddie's Italian Olive Pizza. Eddie makes a delicious cheese and olive pizza for his friends, using homemade dough and cooking it outside in his family's pizza oven.

programmes.show_name: Teeny Tiny Creatures with Chantelle and Rory
programmes.show_description: Starfish. Chantelle meets unusual starfish while Rory and Sandy go litter picking.
Mission: Party Time. Disability dog Tinks is determined to help Charli feel confident enough to go to a party.
Mangshan Pit Viper and White-Blotched River Stingray. Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a pit viper and a white-blotched river stingray.
Waffle's Rehearsal. The Poodles' Class rehearse for the school show, and Waffle has a special line to sing.
Run, Flap, Fly. Oona learns an amazing new skill.
I Love School. Animated series about a group of singing potatoes. Ruby sings a diva-style ballad about the thing she loves more than anything else - school.
The Pop-Ups. Stripes's attempts to write a song about his love for the Sunhouse are interrupted by the rest of the gang. Can Yukee find a way to help him finish his song?
The Radio Badge. The Squirrels find some of Duggee's radio equipment and decide to start their own show.
Bowl. Dee encourages a friend to say 'bowl', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We explore lots of different types of bowls.
The Big Picture. Numberblock Seventeen is hosting a big area art challenge. Numberblocks One, Three and Four need to cover a canvas with shapes - without leaving any gaps - to find the area.
Name. Educational fun. When A gets hiccups, she can't say her own name - but Magic E and friends sing a song to help her remember.
Theme Park Adventure. Justin and his friends ride a rollercoaster, and Mr Tumble sings the Tumble Toys to sleep.
Making Sounds. Preschool fun from Teletubbyland. Po makes silly, funny sounds with the levers and buttons in Home Dome and then the Tubby Phone rings for a silly Tubby Phone Dance.
Patchwork Bison. Patchwork Bison has a problem: he has lost his brother! Will the Patchwork Pals find a way to help him?
Littlest Laugh. Stop-frame animation featuring Raa Raa, who lives in the Jingly Jangly Jungle. Raa Raa and Ooo Ooo try to find out who is the funniest animal in the jungle.
Chug Patrol: Mission Thunder. When a thunder storm comes to Chuggington, Wilson can't control his fear!
Episode 21. Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. It's autumn in Will Barrow's garden, and a cheeky squirrel is finding lots of places to bury his nuts.
Woolly Rhino and Flowers. Adventure series. Andy goes in search of a woolly rhino, travelling back 50,000 years to a snowy, prehistoric mountain range.
Listen to Our Song! The Game Catchers land on the Amusement Park Planet to play freezing ball. But a local alien doesn't want to play because he has had a disagreement with his friends.
Urchin Invasion. Animated deep sea adventures. The Octonauts head to the kelp forest to visit Shellington's sister Pearl and her newborn son Periwinkle.
The Tale of the Surprising Sisters. Animated series. Flopsy and Mopsy insist on accompanying Peter on one of his adventures.
The Opposites Badge. The Squirrels learn what opposites are with the help of some topsy-turvy animal friends!
Zigzag's Snail Service. When Zigzag needs to wear an eye patch, Vida enlists the help of Molasses, the seeing eye snail, but Zigzag refuses.
To Catch a Button. Supertato promises Raisin that he will keep her most precious button, Princess Bella Bob, safe. But Evil Pea plans to take it to ruin Supertato's reputation.
Mission Hide-and-Seek. Weasy cheats at hide-and-seek by putting a GPS on one of the ducklings so he can always find them. This soon backfires when the ducklings catch on to his scheme!
Piggy in the Riddle. Can Mojo and Bo get three puzzling passengers across Lake Swoppiton at once?
Smoochy Kiss. Bluey and Bingo want Dad all to themselves and won't let Mum have a smoochy kiss!
Five Piris and a Little Egg. The Piris look after a special package for Shirley but get more than they bargained for.
Stumpfest. Dad and his mates hit the backyard ready to rip up some tree stumps!
Andy, the T Rex and the Volcano. Andy and Jen embark on an adventure across Dino Island to investigate a mysterious sound.
Waffle and the Birthday Wish. Evie gets a homemade dog house for her birthday, and Mrs Hobbs takes up rollerskating.
The Bedtime Wish  . Rafi's flying bed wish turns bedtime into an adventure. But she realises bedtime is bedtime for a reason when Aunty T magics them safely home.
It's Time for a Spring Clear Out. JoJo and Gran Gran are having a spring clear out, but when Gran Gran wants to clear out her old jumper, JoJo is distraught - she loves that snuggly jumper!
Basking Shark. Animation for preschool children. Sarah is keen to adopt a sea creature, but doesn't get her first choice.
Blob. Stop-motion animation. The Froglets produce a noisy, bouncy purple blob out of the Top Hat. Tiny and Small discover it can split in two, into four, into eight and so on...
Tutu. MeMo and BaBa play dress up. BaBa is sad when a pretty tutu doesn't fit - but it fits MeMo perfectly. MeMo sees that BaBa is sad and finds a way for them to both play with the tutu.
Wave to the Wottingers. Series for young children. The Pontipines set out on their long walk, but everywhere they go their neighbours, the Wottingers, are there before them.
Gok Wan - My Daddies. Join Gok Wan for a celebratory story about one lucky girl and her two delightful daddies.
